sales.title: ANCIENT GREEK, Seleukid Kingdom, AR tetradrachm, Seleukis I Nikator, 312-281 BC, types of Alexander III ("the Great"), Seleukeia on the Tigris Mint (ca. 296/5-281 BC), NGC Ch AU / strike 4/5, surface 4/5, ex-Rutherford. HGC 9, 12I; cf. SC 119.2B; Newell, ESM 15,14. 17.01 grams. Head of Herakles right, wearing lion skin / Zeus seated left on throne, holding scepter and eagle; monogram to left; below throne, BE below throne. Nicely centered with plenty of detail. Pedigreed to the S.P. Rutherford Collection (stated on label).

MACEDON UNDER ROME. Aesillas, as Quaestor (ca. 95-65 BC). AR tetradrachm (31mm, 17.01 gm, 1h). NGC MS S 5/5 - 5/5. Uncertain mint. MAKEΔONΩN, head of Alexander III the Great, with horn of Ammon; Θ behind / AESILLAS, money chest, club and quaestor's chair; Q in right field, all within wreath. SNG Copenhagen 1330. Shimmering luster over light toning. HID05401242017 © 2025 Heritage Auctions | All Rights Reserved www.HA.com/TexasAuctioneerLicenseNotice
Ancients

CARIAN ISLANDS. Cos. Ca. 400-350 BC. AR tetradrachm (24mm, 15.21 gm, 5h). NGC Choice XF 5/5 - 3/5, Fine Style, light marks.  Chian standard, ca. 380-350 BC. Alcimachus, magistrate. Head of Heracles left, wearing lion skin headdress / ΚΩΙΟN, crab, viewed from above; AΛKIMAXOΣ above club right below crab, all in dotted square frame. HGC 6, 1302. Subtle toning in the recesses with dimensional designs.  As a significant participant in the Carian civilization, which thrived from the Bronze Age to the Classical period, Cos was a beacon of culture, intellect, and historical evolution in the southeastern Aegean. Known far and wide for its medical prowess, it hosted the Asclepeion, a sanctuary dedicated to Asclepius, the Greek god of medicine. The island also carries the distinction of being the birthplace of the renowned ancient Greek physician, Hippocrates, affectionately dubbed the 'Father of Medicine'. Cos was ultimately Hellenized, and under Alexander the Great, it became an important naval base. The architecture and artifacts discovered on the island - including a gymnasium, an odeon, Roman villas with intricate mosaics, and the monumental plane tree reputedly planted by Hippocrates himself - serve as enduring symbols to the island's rich historical tapestry.   HID05401242017  © 2025 Heritage Auctions | All Rights Reserved www.HA.com/TexasAuctioneerLicenseNotice

MACEDON UNDER ROME. Aesillas, as Quaestor (ca. 95-65 BC). AR tetradrachm (30mm, 16.44 gm, 12h). NGC Choice XF 4/5 - 4/5. Uncertain mint. MAKEΔONΩN, head of Alexander the Great, with horn of Ammon; Θ behind / AESILLAS, money chest, club and quaestor's chair; Q in right field, all within wreath. SNG Copenhagen 1330. Aesillas served as quaestor in the Macedonian province during the transitional period in which Rome established control of Greece. His coins notably united Macedonian and Roman tradition, blending Hellenistic iconography with the ideals of the Republic - most prominently seen on his silver tetradrachms. HID05401242017 © 2025 Heritage Auctions | All Rights Reserved www.HA.com/TexasAuctioneerLicenseNotice
Ancients

SYRIA. Seleukid Kingdom. Seleukos I Nikator, 312-281 B.C. AR Tetradrachm (16.80 gms), Susa Mint, ca. 305/4-295 B.C. NGC Ch F, Strike: 5/5 Surface: 4/5.
HGC-9, 20; SC-173.14. Obverse: Bust of hero (Alexander or Seleukos?) right, wearing Attic helmet covered with a panther skin and adorned with bull's ear and horns; Reverse: Nike standing right, placing wreath on top of a trophy of arms to her right; to lower left, M; between Nike and trophy, ΑΧ. Perfectly centered on a round flan. Though circulated, this example retains a strong strike with moderate charcoal toning.
From the Peter Jones M.D. Collection (Greek Coin Art pg. 209; #2716).


To view all items from the Peter Jones M.D. Collection, click here.

sales.title:
MACEDON. Kingdom of Macedon. Alexander I, ca. 498-454 B.C. AR Tetradrachm (12.55 gms), ca. 476-460 B.C. NGC EF, Strike: 4/5 Surface: 2/5. Scuffs.
HGC-3.1, 767; SNG ANS-25-26. Obverse: Horseman riding right, wearing kausia and holding spears; Reverse: Forepart of goat right within incuse square. A RARE issue in general, and usually well-worn when encountered. This example, by contrast, preserves a fine strike in sharp attractive condition. A few marks and scuffs, mostly obscured by the coin's mottled gray tone. A high-class representative of this early Macedonian type.
From the Peter Jones M.D. Collection (Greek Coin Art pg. 55; #1261).

Ex: Classical Numismatic Group 73 (9/2006) Lot # 122 (with ticket).
To view all items from the Peter Jones M.D. Collection, click here.
